author: mark
text:
link_add: history fallback for closed non-gates targets

bl link add looked up its target only in the live store at state-branch HEAD. A target archived seconds earlier (closed) was rejected as 'task not found', even though bl show <id> still resolves it via history. Retrospective cross-refs - a fresh ball pointing back at the just-closed parent - had to live in prose instead of the links list, defeating the discoverability promise of links.

When the live lookup misses, fall back to archive_recovery::recover_one (the same engine bl show uses) so non-blocking link types succeed. gates stays rejected against a closed target: a closed gate is a no-op blocker that would let bl close pass spuriously.
